sourceMovie.title: Punch-Drunk Love
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Punch-Drunk Love for the same reason it broke through in 2002: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Paul Thomas Anderson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The romance and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Café Society
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Punch-Drunk Love clicked for you, Café Society (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Punch-Drunk Love?
faq.answer: Try Café Society, Confessions of a Dangerous Mind, The Brothers Bloom—each shares DNA with Punch-Drunk Love while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Punch-Drunk Love?
faq.answer: Punch-Drunk Love (2002) is best described as Romance, Drama, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Punch-Drunk Love worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
